items.title: Why Trump Is Sending So Much Firepower Toward Iran
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The U.S. is surging firepower to the Middle East as President Trump ramps up pressure on Iran over its nuclear program. The U.S. has 12 warships in the region, including the USS Abraham Lincoln that is armed with F-35C and F-18 fighter jets. On land, the U.S. has bases and installations across the Middle East and coordinates with its ally, Israel. 

WSJ’s Shelby Holliday explains the massive U.S. military build up in the region and how Washington is preparing for potential Iranian retaliation. 

#Iran #Trump #WSJ</article>

items.title: The 12 Biggest Tech Things in My 12 Years at the Journal | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>After 12 years with The Wall Street Journal, this is Joanna Stern’s final video as a full-time employee. Before she leaves, Joanna reflects on twelve things she experienced in tech in the last twelve years at the Journal, including the evolution of Apple’s iPhones, the metaverse, the generative AI craze and more. 

Chapters:
0:00 Joanna’s start at WSJ 
0:51 Advancing phone capabilities 
1:07 Technologies dying out 
1:22 Emojis!
1:34 Amazon’s Alexa and Google’s Assistant
1:47 The Apple Watch
2:37 5G speed
3:03 Becoming a public enemy in Korea 
3:10 The Metaverse
3:20 Big interviews in the tech world
3:54 All the home robots 
4:11 EV advancements 
4:21 The generative AI craze
4:48 Joanna signing off at WSJ

Tech Things With Joanna Stern
Everything is now a tech thing. In creative and humorous videos, WSJ senior personal tech columnist Joanna Stern explains and reviews the products, services and trends that are changing our world.

#Tech #AI #WSJ</article>

items.title: Savannah Guthrie Posts Plea for Mother's Return
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>“Today” co-anchor Savannah Guthrie posted a tearful plea on Instagram for the return of her missing mother.⁠
⁠
Nancy Guthrie, 84, was first reported missing in Arizona on Sunday, and the sheriff’s department is treating the disappearance as a crime.⁠
⁠
During a news briefing on Monday, Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos said police saw concerning signs at Nancy Guthrie’s home after she was reported missing. He said that after processing the information at the scene, authorities believe “that we do in fact have a crime scene, that we do in fact have a crime.”⁠
⁠
Nanos has said investigators haven’t identified a suspect or person of interest in the case. The department also said it was aware of reports of possible ransom “note(s).”⁠

#Today #News #WSJ</article>

items.title: Walmart’s CEO Shares How Warren Buffett Hurt His Feelings
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Doug McMillon has been CEO of Walmart for 12 years and worked at the retail behemoth for over three decades. He led the company through a time of rapid growth and significant changes as it adapted to a digital world. 

He sat down with WSJ ahead of his last day to discuss his strategy at Walmart, why Warren Buffett hurt his feelings, how involved CEOs should be in politics and more. 

#Walmart #WarrenBuffett #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Disney Is Betting $60B on This Secret Team
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Theme parks and cruises have overtaken television as Disney’s biggest source of profits, and the company is counting on them to fuel its growth for the rest of this decade and beyond.

#Disney #WaltDisney #WSJ</article>

items.title: Ken Griffin on Why CEOs Are 'Terrified' to Speak Out on Political Issues
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Citadel founder and CEO Ken Griffin said other CEOs were terrified to speak out on social and political issues right now because they fear a social-media-fueled product boycott because of their stances.⁠
⁠
Griffin, a Republican megadonor, emerged last year as President Trump’s most vocal critic on Wall Street. In an interview with WSJ Editor in Chief Emma Tucker at Invest Live, Griffin criticized the Trump administration's interference into the day-to-day business of American companies. ⁠

#Citadel #Politics #WSJ</article>

items.title: Why Trump Is Relying on Coast Guard Special Forces for Key Missions | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>With its special forces playing a key role in Trump’s national security missions, the Coast Guard is standing up a new command for its elite teams. These special forces units have ramped up drug interdictions in the Caribbean and the Pacific, leading seizers of oil tankers.
WSJ’s Shelby Holliday visited a unit in Miami to learn more. 

Chapters: 
0:00 The Coast Guard’s special forces
0:40 Trump's use of these special forces
2:05 Law enforcement authority and training 
3:00 The drills and use of force
4:41 The resource and people demand
